source_name: Humbird et al. (2011) NREL/TP-5100-47764 — standard bioprocess uptime
notes: Annual uptime fraction; 90% is standard NREL nth-plant assumption (8000 h/yr)

source_name: Educated guess — formate electrolysis energy proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: No validated formate electrolyzer dataset exists at scale. Value estimated from CO2-to-formate Faradaic efficiency ~90% at ~3V cell potential; consistent with range 0.12-0.25 kWh/mol in lab-scale CO2 electrolysis literature. Replace when specific cathode data available.

source_name: Educated guess — CO2-to-formate cathode efficiency proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: High-performance Sn/Bi cathodes achieve 80-97% FE for formate at moderate current density. 0.90 is an optimistic but achievable screening value. Fan et al. 2020 Nat Commun reports ~97% FE on Bi catalyst.

source_name: Complementary to formate FE — electrochemical balance
notes: H2 byproduct fraction = 1 - formate FE; used for mass and credit accounting. Actual H2 FE depends on cathode selectivity.

source_name: Electrochemical stoichiometry — 1 mol H2O per mol formate
notes: Water feed for formate synthesis: CO2 + H2O + 2e- -> HCOO- + OH-; MW(H2O) = 18 g/mol

source_name: Electrochemical stoichiometry — MW(CO2)/MW(HCOO-) = 44/46
notes: Fixed by molecular weights; 44.01/46.03 = 0.956. No uncertainty.

source_name: Educated guess — strain-specific screening assumption
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Insufficient published X. autotrophicus GJ10 formate-to-NH3 yield data. Liu et al. 2017 PNAS demonstrated the biohybrid concept but did not report mass yields at scale. Value back-calculated from thermodynamic formate energy content and N2 fixation energy cost; remains a tunable biological assumption.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product yield proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Biomass yield from formate carbon depends on assimilation pathway and maintenance. 4 kg dry SCP per kg NH3 is a rough estimate assuming most fixed carbon goes to biomass while N2 fixation yields NH3 as primary N product. Needs experimental validation.

source_name: Educated guess — stoichiometric proxy anchored to ammonia pathway
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Biological urea production requires 2 mol NH3 + 1 mol CO2 per mol urea. Formate demand estimated from NH3 pathway scaled by stoichiometry. No published data for direct biological urea from formate via X. autotrophicus.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product for bio-urea pathway
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Lower SCP yield than NH3 case because more fixed N and C are diverted to urea. Ratio estimated from mass balance closure. Needs experimental validation.

source_name: Matassa et al. (2016) — HOB biomass composition
notes: N content of bacterial SCP typically 7-12% of dry weight; 8% is conservative for HOB biomass. Matassa et al. 2016 Water Research reports protein content ~70% which implies ~11% N; 8% accounts for lower-protein harvest.

source_name: Standard microbial biomass empirical formula CH1.8O0.5N0.2
notes: Carbon mass fraction 47-52% is standard for bacterial biomass based on empirical formula. 0.50 is representative mid-value widely used in bioprocess engineering.

source_name: Educated guess — vacuum stripping screening value
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Vacuum stripping NH3 recovery 85-95% reported in literature depending on pH and temperature. 92% is mid-range for optimized alkaline conditions. Ref: Soto-Herranz et al. 2021 review of NH3 recovery technologies.

source_name: Educated guess — evaporation-crystallization screening value
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Urea crystallization recovery depends on concentration and solubility. 90% is typical for single-effect evaporative crystallization at moderate feed concentration. No specific bio-urea recovery reference available.

source_name: Educated guess — vacuum stripping electricity proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Literature reports 3-12 kWh/kg-N for vacuum stripping (Soto-Herranz et al. 2021). Converting to per-kg-NH3 basis and using optimistic end gives ~0.5 kWh/kg-NH3. Actual value depends on vacuum depth and heat integration.

source_name: Educated guess — caustic requirement for acid trap
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Stoichiometric NaOH for pH adjustment + acid trap absorption. 1-3 mol NaOH per mol NH3 is typical range. 2.0 is screening mid-value assuming H2SO4 acid trap with partial NaOH for pH.

source_name: Educated guess — membrane contactor electricity proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Membrane stripping is lower energy than vacuum; literature reports <1.2 kWh/kg-N. 0.30 kWh/kg-NH3 is optimistic for gas-permeable HF membrane with acid trap.

source_name: Educated guess — hollow-fiber gas-permeable membrane flux
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: HF membrane NH3 flux range 0.1-1.0 kg/m2/h in literature. 0.50 is mid-range for PTFE hollow-fiber at pH >10 and 40-60C. Used for membrane area and cost sizing.

source_name: Educated guess — heated air stripping electricity proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Air stripping is more energy-intensive than vacuum or membrane; literature reports 5-15 kWh/kg-N. 0.80 kWh/kg-NH3 is optimistic assuming heat recovery and efficient blower.

source_name: Educated guess — single-effect evaporator electricity proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Electricity for pumping and solids handling in evaporative crystallization. Most energy is in steam; 0.12 kWh/kg covers auxiliary electricity. Consistent with NREL convention for evaporator auxiliaries.

source_name: Educated guess — LP steam for single-effect concentration and crystallization
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Single-effect evaporator requires ~1 kg steam per kg water evaporated. With feed at ~10% urea concentration needing ~8 kg water removed per kg urea; multi-effect or heat integration reduces to ~0.65 kg/kg. Conservative screening value.

source_name: Educated guess — fermenter sizing proxy from prior screening models
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: No published volumetric productivity for X. autotrophicus NH3 at scale. Value estimated from typical aerobic fermentation productivities (0.2-1.0 kg/m3/h for various metabolites). Needs experimental validation.

source_name: Educated guess — more conservative than ammonia case
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Biological urea pathway assumed slower than direct NH3 due to additional enzymatic steps. 0.30 is ~70% of NH3 productivity as a conservative screening assumption.

source_name: Educated guess — biorefinery broth dilution proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Fermentation broths are typically 2-15% product concentration. 10 kg water per kg NH3 implies ~10% effective concentration which is reasonable for a high-N broth. Consistent with NREL bioprocess broth conventions.

source_name: Educated guess — urea broth dilution proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Urea is more soluble than NH3 salts so broth may be more concentrated. 8 kg water per kg urea implies ~12.5% concentration. Conservative screening assumption.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP dewatering benchmark
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Centrifugation of bacterial biomass typically yields 15-25% dry solids cake. 0.78 moisture (22% solids) is representative for disc-stack centrifuge on bacterial cells. Matassa et al. 2016 discusses HOB biomass harvesting.

source_name: Standard dried SCP product specification
notes: Final packaged SCP moisture 5-10% is industry standard for dried microbial protein products (comparable to spray-dried fishmeal at 8-10%).

source_name: Educated guess — spray/drum drying energy proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Industrial drying 0.8-1.5 kWh/kg water removed depending on dryer type. 1.15 is representative for combined spray/drum drying. NREL Humbird et al. 2011 uses similar values for biomass drying.

source_name: Educated guess — disc-stack centrifuge energy proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Disc-stack centrifuges typically consume 1-3 kWh/m3 for microbial separations. 1.2 kWh/m3 is representative for bacterial biomass harvest. Consistent with NREL bioprocess conventions.

source_name: Educated guess — combined mixing and aeration energy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Aerobic fermentation aeration 0.5-1.5 kWh/m3/h depending on oxygen demand and reactor geometry. 0.85 is mid-range for highly aerobic autotrophic culture. Consistent with NREL convention.

source_name: Educated guess — compressed air screening assumption
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Electricity to deliver compressed air to bioreactor. Standard air compressor efficiency ~0.1 kWh/kg air; N2 fraction ~78%. 0.05 kWh/kg-N2 is screening approximation.

source_name: Educated guess — stainless steel reactor base cost at 100 m3
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: 100 m3 stainless steel reactor purchase cost 40000-80000 USD based on NREL equipment cost databases and vendor quotes in Humbird et al. 2011. 50000 is mid-range screening value.

source_name: Humbird et al. (2011) NREL/TP-5100-47764 — reference reactor size
notes: Reference reactor size for scaling; NREL uses 100-1000 m3 fermenters in design reports

source_name: Educated guess — ammonia recovery equipment base cost at 1000 kg/day
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Vacuum stripping column + condenser + acid trap at 1000 kg/day NH3. Estimated from NREL distillation/stripping equipment costs scaled to this throughput. Highly uncertain; replace with vendor quote.

source_name: Educated guess — urea recovery equipment base cost at 1000 kg/day
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Single-effect evaporator + crystallizer at 1000 kg/day urea. Slightly higher than NH3 recovery due to evaporation equipment. Replace with vendor quote.

source_name: Educated guess — centrifuge and dryer base cost at 1000 kg/day SCP
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Disc-stack centrifuge + spray dryer at 1000 kg/day SCP. Estimated from NREL solid-liquid separation equipment costs. Replace with vendor quote.

source_name: Peters et al. (2003) Plant Design and Economics for Chemical Engineers — six-tenths rule
notes: Standard 0.6-0.8 scaling exponent for process equipment; 0.70 is widely used in bioprocess screening TEA. NREL Humbird et al. 2011 uses equipment-specific exponents but 0.70 is appropriate for screening.

source_name: Educated guess — hollow-fiber gas-permeable membrane flux for NH3
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: HF membrane NH3 flux range 0.1-1.0 kg/m2/h; duplicate entry kept for backward compatibility with membrane recovery method sizing. See notes on membrane_energy row.

source_name: Educated guess — MAP precipitation mixing and dewatering energy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Struvite precipitation is low-energy (mixing + settling + dewatering). 0.05-0.15 kWh/kg-NH3 consistent with stirred-tank reactor + belt filter press. Huang et al. 2022 (PMC9222832) reviews struvite crystallization parameters.

source_name: Struvite stoichiometry with Mg excess — standard practice
notes: Stoichiometric 1:1 Mg:NH4 ratio with 5% excess to ensure >95% conversion at pH 8.5-9.0. Huang et al. 2022 (PMC9222832) and Le Corre et al. 2009 (Environ Technol) confirm 1.0-1.3 Mg:N optimal range.

source_name: Struvite stoichiometry with P excess — standard practice
notes: Stoichiometric 1:1 PO4:NH4 ratio with 5% excess. H3PO4 used as phosphate source. Consistent with literature optimum Mg:N:P ~ 1.05:1:1.05.

source_name: Struvite N recovery at optimized pH — literature consensus
notes: N recovery as struvite 80-95% at pH 8.5-9.0 depending on competing ions. 88% is mid-range. Huang et al. 2022 reviews kinetics and recovery. PMC12243535 reports 86.5% NH4 removal in fluidized bed.

source_name: Struvite stoichiometry — MW(MgNH4PO4·6H2O) / MW(NH3) = 245.3/17.03
notes: Fixed by molecular weights; 1 mol struvite per mol NH3. No uncertainty in stoichiometry.

source_name: Educated guess — MAP settler and dewatering equipment at 1000 kg/day NH3
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Reaction tank + mixer + settler + belt filter press. Lower CapEx than stripping column. Estimated from NREL crystallizer/settler equipment cost ranges. Highly uncertain; replace with vendor quote.

source_name: Educated guess — MVR compressor electricity from sugar/evaporation industry
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: MVR systems replace steam with electricity via vapor recompression. Sugar industry MVR evaporators use 0.3-0.6 kWh/kg water evaporated. 0.45 kWh/kg-urea accounts for crystallization step. Well-proven at scale in food/chemical industry.

source_name: Educated guess — minimal LP steam for crystal polishing in MVR circuit
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: MVR eliminates 90%+ of steam demand vs single-effect. Only final crystallization polish requires LP steam. 0.05 kg/kg is ~92% steam reduction.

source_name: Educated guess — MVR compressor plus crystallizer at 1000 kg/day urea
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Higher CapEx than single-effect evaporator due to compressor cost; recovered through steam savings at scale. Estimated from industrial MVR equipment cost ranges. Highly uncertain.

source_name: MAP fertilizer stoichiometry — NH3 + H3PO4 -> NH4H2PO4 with slight excess
notes: Monoammonium phosphate synthesis: 1:1 stoichiometry with 2% H3PO4 excess for complete neutralization. Standard industrial practice per Nutrien eKonomics and IFDC technical guides.

source_name: MAP fertilizer stoichiometry — MW(NH4H2PO4)/MW(NH3) x 0.97 recovery
notes: MW(NH4H2PO4) = 115.03; MW(NH3) = 17.03; ratio = 6.754; times 0.97 conversion = 6.55. Fixed by molecular weights and assumed recovery.

source_name: Educated guess — neutralization + granulation equipment at 1000 kg/day NH3
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Neutralization tank + spray dryer or prilling tower. Estimated from NREL reactor/dryer equipment costs. Highly uncertain; replace with vendor quote.

source_name: Educated guess — H2 demand for autotrophic NH3 production via X. autotrophicus
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Derived from ~1.5 g CDW/g H2 biomass yield (C. necator ~2.3 g/g reduced 35% for N2-fixing Xanthobacter) and SCP:NH3 ratio of 3.5. Includes H2 for both growth and nitrogenase energy (8 ATP per N2 fixed). Matassa et al. 2016 Water Research.

source_name: Educated guess — H2 demand for autotrophic urea production
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Lower than NH3 route because urea (MW 60) contains 2 NH3 equivalents but less SCP per kg product. Scaled from h2_to_ammonia proportional to formate-to-urea/formate-to-ammonia ratio.

source_name: CBB cycle stoichiometry — CO2 assimilation per kg biomass
notes: Calvin-Benson-Bassham cycle: 0.44 g H2 + 1.83 g CO2 per g biomass for C. necator (Matassa et al. 2016). Stoichiometric relationship holds for any CBB-using autotroph including Xanthobacter.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product yield on H2/CO2 pathway
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Lower than formate case (4.0) due to higher maintenance energy cost of autotrophic growth and gas-liquid mass transfer limitations. Xanthobacter yields 50-80% of C. necator biomass under N2-fixing conditions.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product yield for urea on H2/CO2
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Scaled from scp_to_ammonia_h2co2 proportional to formate urea/ammonia SCP ratio.

source_name: NREL PEM electrolyzer system energy — 55 kWh/kg H2 at system level
notes: PEM water electrolysis at system level: 50-60 kWh/kg H2 (Badgett et al. 2024 NREL). 0.055 kWh/mol = 55 kWh/kg / 500 mol/kg. Stack efficiency ~70% at 2.0 V cell potential.

source_name: Electrochemical stoichiometry — 1 mol H2O per mol H2
notes: Water electrolysis: H2O -> H2 + 0.5 O2. MW(H2O) = 18 g/mol. Stoichiometric minimum; actual water consumption slightly higher due to cooling.

source_name: Educated guess — autotrophic fermenter sizing proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Gas-liquid mass transfer limits autotrophic productivity vs heterotrophic. ~70% of formate case (0.42). Literature reports 0.2-0.5 kg/m3/h for HOB at moderate gas supply rates.

source_name: Educated guess — autotrophic urea fermenter sizing
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: ~70% of formate urea productivity (0.30). Additional enzymatic steps for urea synthesis reduce overall rate.

source_name: Educated guess — methanol demand for methylotrophic NH3 production
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Biomass yield Yx/s ~0.45 g/g methanol (typical serine/RuMP pathway). With SCP:NH3 ratio of 3.8 kg/kg: methanol = 3.8/0.45 + overhead for N2 fixation energy ~9.5. Dijkhuizen & Harder 1987 report methanol dissimilation in Xanthobacter H4-14.

source_name: Educated guess — methanol demand for methylotrophic urea production
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Scaled from methanol_to_ammonia proportional to formate urea/ammonia ratio (6.5/11.0). Methanol provides both C and energy for urea synthesis.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product yield on methanol pathway
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Methylotrophic growth is heterotrophic; higher yield than H2/CO2 but lower than formate due to methanol oxidation energy loss. Range 3-5 kg SCP/kg NH3 reasonable for screening.

source_name: Educated guess — SCP co-product for methanol urea pathway
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Scaled from scp_to_ammonia_methanol proportional to formate urea/ammonia SCP ratio.

source_name: Educated guess — methylotrophic fermenter sizing proxy
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: Methanol is liquid so no gas-liquid mass transfer limitation. Slightly lower than formate (0.42) due to methanol toxicity requiring fed-batch control. Literature reports 0.3-0.5 kg/m3/h for methylotrophs.

source_name: Educated guess — methylotrophic urea fermenter sizing
notes: EDUCATED GUESS: ~70% of methanol NH3 productivity. Additional enzymatic steps for urea synthesis.
